mechanism_narrative: Sustained low unemployment indicates a strong labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% as of t-1mo is historically low
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative or flat during periods of sustained low unemployment, or the Rank IC is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the federal funds rate above a certain threshold signals monetary tightening, which increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.69% as of t-1mo, indicating ongoing tightening
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust employment growth indicates healthy consumer demand and income, boosting corporate revenues and justifying higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 497559.0 and PAYEMS at 148424.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and PAYEMS show strong growth, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of cases, or the Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth, an acceleration in output signals robust economic activity and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. This effect is amplified in early-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: In early-cycle expansions, periods of INDPRO growth > 0.3% over 3 months do not consistently lead to positive S&P 500 60-day returns, or the signal's alpha is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts signals a weakening in the construction sector and broader economic demand, which can precede a slowdown in corporate earnings and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where HOUST declines by > 10% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low inflation, as measured by CPI, suggests stable pricing power and lower pressure on central banks to tighten, supporting a favorable environment for equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently negative during periods of stable or declining CPIAUCSL, or the Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is rising rapidly while GDP growth remains strong, it suggests an overheating economy that will likely trigger aggressive monetary tightening, leading to a negative impact on S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as discount rates rise.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 4% AND GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth > 3%
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive or flat when both CPIAUCSL and GDPC1 show strong growth, despite subsequent FEDFUNDS hikes, or the Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, which implies reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by > 0.3pp in 1 month.
